title: Seabright Quantum Analytics
preview:
Quick summary: strong technical interest after demo — liked dedicated routing and caching features. CTO said: "latency profiles look great but we need SSO + audit log answers before legal." Asked for architecture diagram and SOC2 evidence. After materials sent (pricing + security FAQ) they went quiet. Possible reasons: internal budget freeze for Q2; primary ML lead on parental leave (no champion). Competitors in play: OpenAI (for hosted simplicity) and Pinecone for vector infra. POC not scheduled.

Sales chatter / next steps (fragments):
- "Try to re-open via 1-pager that maps to their security answers (Ethan)."
- "Offer a 2-week dedicated pilot with limited data residency and audit logs as proof — needs legal OK. (Priya)"
- "If no response by 2026-03-20 -> mark as stalled and transition to nurture sequence."

Stalled tickbox: post-demo no follow-up; likely closed-lost if no re-engagement by forecast month.

title: Arcadia Promptcraft
preview:
Overview: Arcadia Promptcraft builds a VS Code extension that provides streaming completions, inline suggestions and fuzzy signature help for JS/TS + Python. Team is engineering-first; prototype shipped but performance issues at scale.

Primary ask: low-latency streaming + per-extension per-user rate limits. They want median inline suggestion RTT <80ms for local dev workflows and tail P99 <200ms for short completions (<64 tokens). Want to support model/version pinning per-channel so customers can choose a lightweight quantized variant for IDE while defaulting to higher-quality model on demand.

Security: Must integrate with customer SSO (SAML), audit logs for suggestion events, and KMS integration for secrets. Data residency not strict but they want VPC option for enterprise customers.

Metrics/POC success criteria: 1) median suggestion latency <80ms; 2) acceptance rate uplift >25% vs baseline; 3) cost per active dev under target ($X/day, discussed in pricing deck); 4) no regressions in suggestion quality measured by curated eval prompts.

Quote from CTO on call: "If we can't guarantee smooth inline experience at scale, customers churn — latency is everything."

Notes from demo: stream looks good on 5-10 dev scale; on 500+ DAU they saw buffering and token drops. Discussed batching/caching and per-session KV cache to keep prefix latencies low. SE presented alternate: route short inline completions to a quantized code model with aggressive KV caching, fallback to larger model for multi-line completions.

Operational asks: per-extension quotas, dynamic rate limits by org tier, token-level cost telemetry in console, hooks to disable streaming for specific users/orgs.

Proposed next steps: get security packet completed, share POC test harness (drive link), align on SLOs and rate-limit config, sign POC SOW (6 weeks).

title: Crestfall Retail AI
preview:
- Demo attended 2026-02-18: product, eng manager, infra lead present.
- Showed live hosted API flow, routing fallback, and Dedicated latency numbers.
- Ask: low-latency reranking for product pages (p95 < 120ms) + embeddings for faceted search.
- Security was clear on residency + KMS; infra said must run in VPC or private offering; interested in dedicated pool.
- AE asked for evaluation dataset & expected QPS; PG said they'd share but dataset never arrived.
- After demo: multiple follow-up emails + 2 voicemails, no reply.
- 2026-02-25: SE sent security FAQ + sample KMS integration notes (see Drive link).
- 2026-03-01: procurement mentioned vendor shortlist; AE heard rumor they favored GoliathAI on price + existing relationship.
- Quote from Product Head on demo: "very promising but we need legal + security to sign off — timeline unclear."
- Current status: unresponsive post-demo. Likely internal reprioritization or champion moved.
- Suggested next moves: one concise value email (ROI and risk mitigation), include estimated cost delta vs GoliathAI, offer 2-week pilot on Dedicated with strict data controls.
- Risk flags: no champion, procurement bias, security still has unanswered questions around key management and residency timing.
- Notes cadence: AE attempted 2/20, 2/24, 3/03; no replies.
- Sales comment: keep in follow-up cadence but deprioritize active pursuit until re-engage or until next product release that maps to their ask.

title: Northlake Customer Support AI
preview:
Account summary (Northlake Customer Support AI)

Firmographics / context
- Mid-market support platform building AI agent add-on for live chat + email. They sell to SaaS + fintech; have a few customers that demand tighter audit + retention controls.
- Stack today: mix of vendor LLM APIs + some self-hosted experimentation. They want to standardize on one inference platform and stop hand-tuning batching.
- Deployment: prefers VPC for production (customer expectations + data isolation). Open to starting hosted API for POC.

Primary use case
- Support-agent chat (streaming) with tool calling.
  - High sensitivity to time-to-first-token (TTFT) and streaming smoothness (chunk cadence).
  - They explicitly said: "If the agent pauses for 2–3 seconds mid-stream, our users think it broke." 
- Secondary: summarization of long tickets, embeddings + reranking for internal KB retrieval.

Requirements (captured across discovery + demo)
- Latency
  - Streaming endpoints are the critical path. They asked for a clear 'FAST' tier that they can set-and-forget.
  - Targets they mentioned (not formally committed yet): TTFT < ~350–500ms p95 (region-dependent), and p99 "no multi-second stalls" during decode.
  - Concern: under bursts, they see tail latency spikes with other providers (queue depth grows; token streaming becomes bursty).
- Traffic shape
  - Concurrency: normal 50–120 concurrent chats; peak events 300–600 concurrent (product launches / incident storms).
  - Prompt lengths: mix of short chat turns + occasional long context (ticket history + KB snippets). They admitted their worst-case prompts are "ugly" (copy/paste logs).
  - Mix: streaming chat dominates daytime; summarization batch jobs off-hours.
- Cost
  - Cost sensitive but not at the expense of perceived responsiveness. They like the idea of cost-efficient tier for async summarization, but want guardrails.
  - Asked whether tier defaults can be applied per-route (streaming chat fast; summarization cost-efficient).
- Model preferences
  - They’re model-agnostic but asked about strong 8B/70B class options for agent chat; they want fallback behavior if a model family is constrained.
  - They care about output stability during rollouts; asked how Redwood detects regressions when changing batching defaults.
- Controls / ergonomics
  - Key request: "Fast as the default for streaming." They do not want to tune max_batch_tokens / wait_ms knobs directly.
  - Wants visibility into the effective profile + why it was chosen (override vs default vs overload mode). Reason codes resonated.
  - Wants an easy override mechanism for enterprise: route-level policy in Console, plus a header/SDK param for experiments.

Security / compliance notes
- Asked for: SSO/SAML for Console, audit logs (who changed policies/tiers), retention controls, and KMS integration for any stored config/telemetry.
- Confirmed that tier selection should not change data handling/logging; they were sensitive to "does fast tier log more?" Answer: no; runtime behavior only.

Buying / stakeholders
- Champion: Head of Product (agent experience) + platform lead (infra). Procurement will care about VPC + SLA.
- They want a concrete rollout story: start hosted, then Dedicated VPC once metrics prove out.

Open questions / follow-ups
- Need their real workload stats: prompt-length distribution (prefill tokens), response length distribution (decode tokens), and streaming on/off split per route.
- Need to align on which SLO tier maps to which endpoints (fast for streaming agent chat; standard for internal tools; cost-efficient for async summarization).
- They asked for guidance on overload behavior per tier (shed load vs increase batching). We should propose a default overload policy for their agent route and show what happens at peak.

Risks
- Mixed workloads on same capacity: they may run streaming chat and summarization concurrently; if they do, we need clear route separation or policy precedence to avoid chat p99 getting dragged.
- Tail latency expectations are aggressive; we should set expectations with published tier definitions and highlight that fast-tier protects tail via tighter wait budgets + admission control.

Next steps (agreed)
1) Northlake sends: 7-day traffic shape snapshot (tokens in/out, prompt buckets, concurrency over time, streaming ratio).
2) Redwood (Ethan Park) provides: POC plan + how to force FAST tier for streaming endpoints (include SDK/header example) and how to monitor TTFT + p99.
3) Redwood to propose Dedicated VPC sizing + override/precedence approach for peak-hour protection.
4) Schedule security checklist review (SSO/SAML, audit log export, retention controls) once POC metrics are positive.

title: Lucid Harbor Analytics
preview:
Attended demo 2026-02-18 — positive reaction to routing/fallback and prefix caching. CISO attended and specifically asked for:
- SOC2 Type II evidence and a KMS/HSM integration diagram.
- Data residency guarantee for EU policies (they have EU customers).
Customer said they'd run a security review and revert; we scheduled a 2/25 sync that was cancelled by them.
Post-demo engagement: 2 follow-up emails (pricing + POC template), one voicemail. No answer.
Signals from contacts: product lead liked cost/latency profile. CISO cautious — "we can't go forward without KMS and retention controls in the contract."
Sales notes: possible loss scenarios = budget freeze (finance paused new vendor spend for Q2) or selection of competitor with stronger enterprise pack (they were demoing Anthropic enterprise as well).
AE actions taken: shared SOC2 link, KMS architecture doc, and redwood security FAQ (drive). Offered a short security deep-dive with infra team (SE + solutions architect).
Current status: cold. Recommended status = nurture; revisit after Q2 budget review or if security team asks for technical deep-dive.
Next tangible: AE to send one exec-level email week of 2026-03-15 asking for decision or permission to close as lost.

title: Sienna Crest Commerce
preview:
Demo completed 2026-02-18. Attendees: Head of Product (Maya Lin), Eng Manager (Tom Alvarez), Security lead (Rebecca Ho), AE Jordan Blake, SE Priya Natarajan. They were enthusiastic about latency and the reranking demo (specifically preferring model variants for cost control). Key quote from Maya: 'Sharp latency and predictable cost are table stakes — we need ironclad security sign-off before pilots.' Security asked for SOC2 evidence, KMS integration diagram, and an answer about audit log retention. We provided SOC2 summary and KMS architecture (drive links above) during the demo.

Post-demo status: two follow-up emails and one Loom sent (02/19, 02/24, 03/03). No reply from Maya or Rebecca. AE left voicemail 03/05; LinkedIn message 03/09. SE offered a 2-week pilot with a dedicated SE hours (no-charge) to unblock eval; haven’t heard back. Prod team appears to have paused new pilots due to budget freeze (internal FYQ re-plan) per a short note from procurement on 02/27. No clear internal champion identified — Maya is stretched and ownership appears to have shifted to a new head of platform (not in attendee list).

Technical requirements recap: latency p95 target <150ms for chat/rerank, peak traffic ~120 QPS for search queries, embeddings throughput for nightly indexing. Model preference: open model with ability to route to cheaper quantized variants for rerank; fallbacks to smaller models during peak. Must support VPC deployment or dedicated private endpoint; require SSO/SAML, KMS for key management, and 1 year audit log retention. Cost sensitivity: high — wants clear unit economics and suggestions on batching/quantization.

Risk assessment: stalled-to-cold. High risk of closed-lost unless a new champion surfaces or procurement budget re-opens. Competitors actively engaged (we saw a follow-up from OpenAI reps on 02/26 per Maya's note).

Action items so far: delivered pricing deck and technical annex (drive links), scheduled security deep-dive offer (SE available week of 03/15), proposed 2-week pilot credit. Recommendation: preserve as warm but deprioritized — attempt one more high-value touch (security office hours + tailored pilot T&Cs) then move to nurture drip (quarterly check-ins).

title: Northstar Support Platform
preview: Northstar is evaluating Redwood to power an LLM-based support agent assist + summarization feature. Key decision criteria: (1) onboarding speed and clarity (TTFS/TTFP), (2) production-safe defaults for streaming + retries/timeouts, (3) predictable cost controls (quotas/budgets + usage analytics), (4) model pinning + fallback routing, and (5) security posture (SSO/SAML, audit logs, US data residency, retention controls, KMS). They prefer VPC deployment but may start on Hosted API if security review can proceed in parallel.

They prefer VPC deployment but may start on Hosted API if security review can proceed in parallel.

title: Kythera MedSearch
preview:
Post-demo silence. Demo attended and product fit was positive (search + rerank + chat for clinicians). Key asks: HIPAA controls, KMS, on-prem option or VPC, data residency in us-east, tokenization/PII redaction. They explicitly asked for quantized model recommendations and cost estimate for 1000 qps inference (typical clinician load).

Current state: Legal/InfoSec placed hold — heavy backlog + demands for SOC2 report copies and encryption-at-rest details. Internal champion (Head of Data Science) changed teams last month -> no clear owner to push pilot. Budget conversation paused due to org-wide hiring/budget freeze through Q2.

Competitive pressure: they have an existing relationship with Azure and are evaluating Anthropic for safety features. Prospect comment on demo: "liked the observability and rollout features" but: "we need security sign-off before procurement."

Suggested approach: send tailored security packet (SOC2 + architecture + KMS playbook), offer 6-week POC credits + technical onboarding support, loop in Director for exec-to-exec nudging. Leave cadence: 1x/month nurture with value emails, re-open in 2025-06.
